description: Fix extension auto-reload from server side, remove version mismatch from extension, add outdated plugins badge in side panel, fix search filter inconsistency, improve failed plugin UX, fix tab state hint, add format:check to check command, auto-build e2e plugin, optimize CI, add changelog automation.

userStories.title: Server-triggered extension auto-reload on update
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Chrome extension to automatically reload when the MCP server detects an updated extension version, so I never need to manually reload from chrome://extensions/.
acceptanceCriteria: When the MCP server starts and extension-install.ts detects a version change (copies new extension files), it sends an extension.reload command to the connected extension via WebSocket
The extension's message-router.ts already handles extension.reload by calling chrome.runtime.reload() — this existing mechanism is reused
If the extension is not connected when the server starts, the reload happens on next WebSocket connection (the server marks a pending reload flag)
The CLI start.ts no longer prints 'Reload the extension from chrome://extensions/ for changes to take effect.' since it's automatic
The existing /extension/reload HTTP endpoint continues to work for manual triggers
userStories.notes:
The extension already supports `extension.reload` via message-router.ts (lines 182-205) — it calls `chrome.runtime.reload()`. The server just needs to trigger this.

Changes needed:

1. **platform/mcp-server/src/extension-install.ts**: `ensureExtensionInstalled()` currently returns `{ installed: boolean; version: string }`. Add a `versionChanged: boolean` flag to the return value. Set it to `true` when files are copied (version mismatch).

2. **platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts**: Add `pendingExtensionReload: boolean` to `ServerState`, initialized to `false`.

3. **platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts** (or wherever server startup orchestration happens): After `ensureExtensionInstalled()`, if `versionChanged` is true, set `state.pendingExtensionReload = true`. If the extension WebSocket is already connected, send the reload command immediately and reset the flag.

4. **platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts** (or extension-protocol.ts): In the WebSocket `open` handler (createHandleWsOpen), after the extension connects, check `state.pendingExtensionReload`. If true, send the `extension.reload` JSON-RPC message and reset the flag. Use a short delay (500ms) to let the extension fully initialize before reloading.

5. **platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts**: Remove the 'Reload the extension...' message from lines 79-80 in autoInitialize. Replace with a message like 'Extension will auto-reload.' (dim).

The extension.reload flow: server sends `{ jsonrpc: '2.0', method: 'extension.reload', id }` via WebSocket -> message-router handles it -> clears wsConnected from storage -> calls chrome.runtime.reload() after 100ms flush delay.

userStories.title: Remove version mismatch banner from side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I no longer need the version mismatch banner since the server auto-reloads the extension when versions differ.
acceptanceCriteria: All references to versionMismatch state in App.tsx are removed
The protocolVersion check in loadPlugins is removed
The SIDE_PANEL_PROTOCOL_VERSION constant is removed from constants.ts if it exists there
The side panel renders without any version mismatch UI
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:

1. **Delete** platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/VersionMismatchBanner.tsx
2. **platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx**:
   - Remove `versionMismatch` from useState (line 28)
   - Remove the protocolVersion check in loadPlugins (lines 66-70)
   - Remove the VersionMismatchBanner import and rendering (around line 219)
   - Remove `versionMismatch: false` from disconnect handler
3. **platform/browser-extension/src/constants.ts**: Remove `SIDE_PANEL_PROTOCOL_VERSION` if defined there
4. Check if any E2E tests reference version mismatch and update them

Also check bridge.ts ConfigStateResult — the protocolVersion field can remain in the server response (backwards compatible), the side panel just no longer reads it.

userStories.title: Show outdated plugins badge in side panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a notification badge when plugin updates are available, like an iOS-style red badge count.
acceptanceCriteria: A new state variable 'outdatedPlugins' is tracked in App.tsx from the fetchConfigState response
When outdatedPlugins.length > 0, a red circular badge with the count is shown in the top-right corner of the side panel header
The badge looks like an iOS notification badge: small red circle with white number, positioned at top-right
Clicking the badge shows a dropdown or expandable section listing each outdated plugin with: name, current version -> latest version, and the npm update command
The update command is rendered in a code block that can be copied
The badge is hidden when outdatedPlugins is empty
userStories.notes:
The data is already available. In bridge.ts, `fetchConfigState()` returns `ConfigStateResult` which includes `outdatedPlugins`. The App.tsx loadPlugins function receives this data but currently ignores it.

1. **platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx**: Add `outdatedPlugins` state (type from shared: `OutdatedPlugin[]`). Set it from `fetchConfigState()` result. Clear it on disconnect.

2. Create **platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/OutdatedPluginsBadge.tsx**: A new component that:
   - Shows a small red circle with count (absolute positioned top-right)
   - On click, opens a popover/dropdown listing outdated plugins
   - Each entry: plugin name, currentVersion -> latestVersion, code block with updateCommand
   - Use the retro design system (Alert variant, or a custom popover)

3. In App.tsx render, place the badge near the top of the layout (in the header area, near the search bar).

The OutdatedPlugin type from server state has: `{ name: string, currentVersion: string, latestVersion: string, updateCommand: string }`. The updateCommand is already formatted as `npm update -g <pkg>` (from version-check.ts).

For the badge style: a `position: absolute` or `relative` container with a red circle. Use Tailwind: `absolute -top-1 -right-1 bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground rounded-full w-5 h-5 text-xs flex items-center justify-center font-bold`.

userStories.title: Fix search filter inconsistency between PluginList and PluginCard
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tool search filter to check the same fields at both the plugin level and the tool level so tools aren't unexpectedly hidden.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginList.tsx and PluginCard.tsx both filter tools by the same fields: displayName, name, and description
PluginList uses displayName, name, and description to determine if a plugin has matching tools (currently only checks name and description)
A tool matching only on displayName is correctly shown in its plugin card and the plugin card is correctly shown in the list
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginList.tsx lines 20-28. Currently:
```typescript
const filtered = plugins.filter(p =>
  p.tools.some(t => t.name.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower) || t.description.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower)),
);
```

Change to also check displayName:
```typescript
const filtered = plugins.filter(p =>
  p.tools.some(t =>
    t.displayName.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower) ||
    t.name.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower) ||
    t.description.toLowerCase().includes(filterLower),
  ),
);
```

This matches what PluginCard.tsx already does in its tool filtering (line ~96-104).

userStories.title: Improve failed plugin card UX
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see failed plugin errors clearly and be able to copy the error text for troubleshooting.
acceptanceCriteria: Failed plugin cards show the error message at a readable font size (at least 12px/text-xs instead of 11px)
The error message is selectable/copyable (user-select: text)
Long error messages are truncated with an expand/collapse toggle
The full error is shown when expanded

userStories.title: Fix tab state hint for unavailable state
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'unavailable' tab state hint to be accurate when the readiness check is not about authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: The unavailable hint changes from 'Log in to {displayName}' to 'Waiting for {displayName} to be ready' or similar generic phrasing
The hint does not assume authentication is the only reason for unavailability
The ready and closed hints remain unchanged
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx TabStateHint component (lines 24-38). Change the unavailable case from:
```
'Log in to {displayName}'
```
To something more generic:
```
'{displayName} is not ready — ensure you are logged in and the page is fully loaded'
```
Or shorter: `'Waiting for {displayName} to be ready'`

The 'closed' hint ('Open {domain} in your browser') is fine as-is. The 'ready' hint (no hint) is fine.

userStories.title: Add format:check to bun run check command
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want format:check included in the unified check command so formatting issues are caught locally.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'check' script in root package.json includes format:check
format:check runs after lint (since lint:fix may change formatting)
userStories.notes:
Edit root package.json. Current scripts:
```
"check": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run test"
"check:all": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run knip && bun run test && bun run test:e2e"
```

Add format:check after lint:
```
"check": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check && bun run knip && bun run test"
"check:all": "bun run build && bun run type-check && bun run lint && bun run format:check && bun run knip && bun run test && bun run test:e2e"
```

userStories.title: Auto-build e2e-test plugin in test:e2e
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the e2e-test plugin to be automatically built before E2E tests run so I don't get cryptic failures on first run.
acceptanceCriteria: The test:e2e script in root package.json builds the e2e-test plugin before running Playwright
The build is conditional — only runs if dist/tools.json is missing or older than src/ files (to avoid unnecessary rebuilds)
If the plugin build fails, the error is clear and the E2E tests do not start
The global-setup.ts prerequisite check still exists as a safety net
CI E2E job also benefits from this change
userStories.notes:
Simplest approach: modify the test:e2e script in root package.json to include the plugin build:

From:
```
"test:e2e": "bunx playwright test"
```

To:
```
"test:e2e": "bun run build:e2e-plugin && bunx playwright test"
```

And add:
```
"build:e2e-plugin": "cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build"
```

For the conditional build: checking file timestamps adds complexity. Simpler to just always build — the plugin build is fast (tsc + opentabs-plugin build is <3s). The `bun install --frozen-lockfile` is also fast if deps are already installed.

Alternatively, wrap in a script that checks if dist/tools.json exists and skip if so. But the simpler always-build approach is more reliable.

userStories.title: Optimize CI with shared build artifacts
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want CI to build once and share artifacts across jobs to reduce redundant work.
acceptanceCriteria: A single 'build' job runs first and uploads the built artifacts
All other jobs (type-check, lint, knip, unit-tests, e2e-tests) download the artifacts instead of building from scratch
The format job remains independent (does not need build artifacts)
Total CI wall time is reduced by eliminating 5 redundant builds
userStories.notes:
Edit .github/workflows/ci.yml. Restructure from 7 independent jobs to a dependency graph:

1. **build** job: runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile && bun run build`, then uses `actions/upload-artifact@v4` to upload the workspace (or at least the `platform/*/dist/` directories and `node_modules/.cache`).

2. **type-check**, **lint**, **knip**, **unit-tests**, **e2e-tests** jobs: add `needs: [build]`, use `actions/download-artifact@v4` to restore build artifacts, skip `bun run build`.

3. **format** job: remains independent (no build dependency).

For the artifact, upload:
- All `platform/*/dist/` directories
- `node_modules/` (or use the bun cache)
- `~/.opentabs/extension/` (for E2E tests)

Actually, the simplest approach: upload the entire workspace as an artifact. With `actions/upload-artifact@v4` compression, this is typically fast.

Alternative simpler approach: just add `needs: [build]` and use bun's build cache. Since tsc --build uses .tsbuildinfo files and bun caches node_modules, the second `bun run build` in dependent jobs would be nearly instant (incremental). But this still runs the build command — just faster.

The cleanest approach: upload `platform/*/dist/` + `platform/browser-extension/dist/` + `.opentabs/extension/` + `bun.lock` as artifacts. Dependent jobs download and skip the build step entirely.

userStories.title: Add automated changelog generation to publish script
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want the publish script to auto-generate a changelog from commits since the last release.
acceptanceCriteria: The publish script generates a CHANGELOG section from git commits since the last tag
The changelog is appended to a CHANGELOG.md file (created if not exists)
Each entry shows the commit subject line, grouped by conventional commit type if available
If no conventional commit format is used, commits are listed ungrouped
The changelog section is included in the release commit
userStories.notes:
Edit scripts/publish.sh. Add a changelog generation step before the git commit.

```bash
echo ""
echo "==> Generating changelog..."

PREV_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -n "$PREV_TAG" ]; then
  COMMITS=$(git log --oneline "$PREV_TAG..HEAD" --no-merges)
else
  COMMITS=$(git log --oneline --no-merges)
fi

# Prepend to CHANGELOG.md
CHANGELOG_ENTRY="## v$VERSION\n\n$(echo "$COMMITS" | sed 's/^[a-f0-9]* /- /')\n"

if [ -f CHANGELOG.md ]; then
  echo -e "$CHANGELOG_ENTRY\n$(cat CHANGELOG.md)" > CHANGELOG.md
else
  echo -e "# Changelog\n\n$CHANGELOG_ENTRY" > CHANGELOG.md
fi

echo "  Generated changelog for v$VERSION"
```

Then add CHANGELOG.md to the git add step:
```bash
git add platform/*/package.json CHANGELOG.md
```
